raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "An estimated 75-92% of lithium-ion batteries are disposed of improperly.",
    "65% of fires in materials recovery facilities in California were attributed to discarded batteries, with 40% of those identified as lithium-ion batteries.",
    "California classifies batteries as hazardous waste and bans them from solid waste landfills.",
    "Improperly disposed batteries pose serious fire, health, and safety hazards.",
    "The influx of improperly disposed batteries has resulted in an alarming number of fires in material recovery facilities, waste collection trucks, and landfills.",
    "Batteries contain hazardous metals and corrosive materials that can poison the environment.",
    "California needs a convenient and efficient system for used battery collection and sorting to reduce toxic environmental impacts.",
    "Improperly disposed lithium-ion batteries can explode and ignite fires if they are not disposed of properly.",
    "It is estimated that 65% of California's waste fires have been sparked by lithium-ion batteries.",
    "The Responsible Battery Recycling Act will establish a long overdue battery collection and recycling program."
  ]
}
```
